name: Refried Bean and Monterey Jack Quesadillas w/ Pickled Onion
recipeIngredient: 1 medium red onion, cut into thin slices
salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
1 cup canned pinto beans, rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 500 degrees F.
2. In a saucepan combine the red onion, vinegar, pickling spices, and salt. Bring to a simmer. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring occasionally. Transfer mixture to a bowl and chill.
3. In a heavy skillet heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over moderately high heat. Cook the onion until tender and golden. Add cumin, salt, and pepper. Cook until the aroma of the cumin is released, about 1 minute. Add beans and water. Cook, stirring and mashing until beans are coarsely mashed, about 5 minutes. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if necessary.
4. Brush one side of two tortillas with remaining oil and place, oil-side down, on a baking sheet. Divide the bean mixture between the two tortillas and sprinkle each with a third cup of cheese. Cover with the remaining two tortillas and lightly brush with oil.
5. Bake quesadillas in the middle of the oven until golden, about 8 to 10 minutes. Sprinkle remaining cheese over each quesadilla and continue baking until melted.
6. Transfer quesadillas to a cutting board and cut into 6 wedges. Top each wedge with a small dollop of sour cream, some pickled onion, and a sprig of cilantro.
Yield: 4 servings
